title: Catamaran Hire for Groups in Puerto Banus: 2026 Costs
html:
Catamaran hire for groups in Puerto Banus starts at roughly EUR 600 for a half day, with most boats leaving from the marina at Puerto Banus or nearby Benabola dock. On BoatHire24 you can book a stable, wide-deck catamaran that carries a sizeable party in comfort along the Costa del Sol.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Most group catamarans cast off from the heart of the resort and then track west or east along the coast, so it helps to know your options. The water here is sheltered for much of the year, which suits larger parties who want a steady deck.

  • Puerto Banus Marina: the main departure point, lined with berths beside the Benabola quay.
  • Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo): a short hop east, useful when Banus berths are full.
  • Puerto Cabopino: a smaller harbour roughly 12 nautical miles east, near the Artola dunes.
  • Estepona Marina: west along the coast, a calmer base for longer group days.
  • La Bajadilla: Marbella's fishing and leisure port, handy for pickups.

Which catamaran should a group rent in Puerto Banus?

For groups, a sailing or power catamaran of 40 to 53 feet is the natural choice, since the twin hulls give a flat, roomy deck and shade for a dozen guests. A deck layout like the one on the Zambezia charter page shows how much lounging space a wider beam adds for a celebration.

  1. Leopard 40 catamaran for compact day groups, around 10 to 11 guests, from EUR 550.
  2. Leopard 43 catamaran for family parties, about 12 guests, from EUR 650.
  3. Leopard 51 catamaran for big celebrations, up to 12 cruising guests, from EUR 1,100.
  4. Leopard 53 catamaran for premium days, broad shaded decks for a dozen guests, from EUR 1,300.
  5. Power catamaran for a faster coastal run, roughly 10 guests, from EUR 900.

How much does it cost to rent a catamaran for a group in Puerto Banus?

A group catamaran day in Puerto Banus typically runs from about EUR 600 for a half day to EUR 2,500 for a large crewed boat in peak season. Prices rise in midsummer and ease in spring and autumn, and many owners list skippered charters in Puerto Banus so a captain handles the day while your party relaxes. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own.

  • Shared catamaran tour: from about EUR 35 per person for a few hours.
  • Small motorboat half-day for a small group: from about EUR 300 in EUR.
  • Catamaran full-day group charter: from about EUR 1,200 to EUR 2,000 in EUR.
  • Crewed yacht for a large party: from about EUR 2,500 per day in EUR.

Do you need a licence to hire a group catamaran in Puerto Banus?

For a larger group catamaran in Spain you will almost always take a skipper, so no personal boating licence is required of the guests. Spain restricts who may helm certain vessels by length and engine power, and the operator confirms the exact requirements for each boat at booking. A crewed charter is the simplest route for a celebration.

High season on the Costa del Sol runs from June to September, when warm, settled days are common. Watch for the Levante, an easterly wind that can build a short chop in the afternoon, so a morning start often gives the calmest water.

How booking a group catamaran works

Booking is straightforward: pick your dates, choose a catamaran sized to your party, and send a request to the owner or skipper. You confirm the meeting point in the marina and settle any fuel or extras directly with the operator before you sail.

At a glance: group catamaran hire in Puerto Banus

  • Price band: from EUR 600 half day to EUR 2,500 full crewed day.
  • Peak months: June to September.
  • Trip length: half day around 4 hours, full day around 8 hours.
  • Capacity: typically 10 to 12 guests per catamaran.
  • Currency: EUR.
  • Year reference: 2026.

Questions about catamaran hire for groups in Puerto Banus

How many people fit on a group catamaran?

Most day catamarans in Puerto Banus carry between 10 and 12 guests comfortably, with the larger Leopard 51 and 53 models offering broad decks and shade that suit a celebration well.

Can we go dolphin watching on a group trip?

Dolphins are seen regularly off the Costa del Sol, and many group catamaran routes head into the bay where pods often appear, though sightings are never guaranteed on a single trip.

How far can a group catamaran cruise in a day?

On a full day a group catamaran can comfortably reach Marbella or Estepona, and some parties track around 12 nautical miles east toward Cabopino, depending on wind, sea state and the skipper's plan.

Is food and drink allowed on board?

Most owners welcome guests bringing food and soft drinks, and many crewed catamarans can arrange catering or a cooler on request, so it is worth confirming details with your operator when you book.

A group catamaran in Puerto Banus turns a birthday, reunion or summer outing into a relaxed day on the Mediterranean, with room to spread out and a skipper to handle the rest. Browse dates, compare decks and book your party afloat through the BoatHire24 marketplace when your group is ready to sail.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
